annotations.comment: capa:doc=One lowercase hex character for a nibble in 0..15.
capa:doc=Encode a byte list as a lowercase hex String, two chars per byte.

capa:doc=Rotate a 32-bit word right by `n` bits. `x` is assumed already
masked to 32 bits; `(x << (32 - n))` can reach 2^63 only if x had
high bits, so the whole expression is re-masked to 32 bits.
capa:doc=Logical right shift of an already-masked 32-bit word.

capa:doc=The 64 round constants (first 32 bits of the fractional parts of
the cube roots of the first 64 primes).

capa:doc=Read one 32-bit big-endian word from `block` starting at byte `i`.
Each byte is in 0..255; the top byte is masked to 8 bits before
the 24-bit shift so the product stays well under i64.
capa:doc=Build the 64-word message schedule for one 64-byte block.

capa:doc=One block of the compression function. Returns the updated state
(the eight working words added back into the incoming state).

capa:doc=SHA padding: append 0x80, then zeros, then the 64-bit big-endian
bit length, to a multiple of 64 bytes. Returns a fresh byte list.

capa:doc=Run the full hash over `message` starting from `iv`, returning the
eight 32-bit state words.
capa:doc=Serialize `count` state words as big-endian bytes (4 each).

capa:doc=SHA-256 digest of `message` as 32 raw bytes.
`message`: bytes, each in 0..255; out-of-range elements are
silently masked to 8 bits (`& 0xFF`). See the BYTE CONTRACT above.

capa:doc=SHA-256 digest of `message` as a 64-character lowercase hex String.
`message`: bytes, each in 0..255; out-of-range elements are
silently masked to 8 bits (`& 0xFF`). See the BYTE CONTRACT above.
capa:doc=SHA-256 of a String's UTF-8 bytes, as lowercase hex.

capa:doc=SHA-224 digest of `message` as 28 raw bytes (the first seven
state words; the eighth is dropped).
`message`: bytes, each in 0..255; out-of-range elements are
silently masked to 8 bits (`& 0xFF`). See the BYTE CONTRACT above.

capa:doc=SHA-224 digest of `message` as a 56-character lowercase hex String.
`message`: bytes, each in 0..255; out-of-range elements are
silently masked to 8 bits (`& 0xFF`). See the BYTE CONTRACT above.
capa:doc=SHA-224 of a String's UTF-8 bytes, as lowercase hex.
